source_pr_title: docs(agents): Cursor Cloud dev environment setup instructions
author: greptile-apps
body: The hello-world example uses `dev-adt.example.com` as the fixture base URL, but AGENTS.md Rule 2 lists the approved fixture hostnames as `DEV` (alias) and `dev-ms.example.com`. An agent following this guide would create a destination pointing to an unapproved hostname, diverging from the established convention used across the rest of the codebase. ```suggestion --config /tmp/openadt.toml --alias DEV --profile sso --transport http \ --auth browser-sso --base-url https://dev-ms.example.com --client 100 --language EN ``` **Context Used:** AGENTS.md ([source](https://app.greptile.com/pepl/github/abapify/openadt/-/custom-context?memory=baeecfb1-1bc0-454f-97c8-315cd1a64a07)) Note: If this suggestion doesn't match your team's coding style, reply to this and let me know. I'll remember it for next time!

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "== AGENTS.md around lines 180-210 =="
nl -ba AGENTS.md | sed -n '175,210p'

echo
echo "== rg: occurrences of 'VM update' and 'update vm' (case-insensitive) =="
rg -n -i "vm[[:space:]]*update|update[[:space:]]*vm|vm.*update|update.*vm" -S --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**'

echo
echo "== fd: files with 'vm' and 'update' in the name (common script extensions) =="
fd -i --type file ".*(vm.*update|update.*vm).*" -e sh -e bash -e cmd -e ps1 -e zsh -e fish -e pwsh -e make -e mk -e bat || true

echo
echo "== fd: likely candidates (names containing vm or update; filter later) =="
fd -i --type file ".*vm.*" -e sh -e bash -e ps1 -e pwsh -e cmd -e bat . | head -n 50 || true
fd -i --type file ".*update.*" -e sh -e bash -e ps1 -e pwsh -e cmd -e bat . | head -n 50 || true
```

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "== AGENTS.md around lines 180-210 =="
awk 'NR>=175 && NR<=210 {printf "%d\t%s\n", NR, $0}' AGENTS.md

echo
echo "== rg: occurrences of 'VM update' / 'update vm' (case-insensitive) =="
rg -n -i "vm[[:space:]]*update|update[[:space:]]*vm|vm.*update|update.*vm" -S --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**' || true

echo
echo "== fd: files with 'vm' and 'update' in the name =="
# fd args: filter by name via regex isn't consistently supported, so do broader 'vm' then filter
fd -i --type file -e sh -e bash -e ps1 -e zsh -e fish -e pwsh -e cmd -e make -e mk -e bat ".*vm.*" . 2>/dev/null | head -n 200 || true
fd -i --type file -e sh -e bash -e ps1 -e zsh -e fish -e pwsh -e cmd -e make -e mk -e bat ".*update.*" . 2>/dev/null | head -n 200 || true
```

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "== repo top-level (for context) =="
ls -la | head -n 200

echo

if [ -d scripts ]; then
  ls -la scripts | head -n 200
else

fi

echo
echo "== fd: candidate update scripts in common dirs (names) =="
fd -i --type f ".*update.*" -e sh -e bash -e ts -e js -e py -e cmd -e ps1 -e zsh -e fish . 2>/dev/null | head -n 200
fd -i --type f ".*vm.*" -e sh -e bash -e ts -e js -e py -e cmd -e ps1 -e zsh -e fish . 2>/dev/null | head -n 200

echo
echo "== rg: likely textual references =="
rg -n --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**' -S -i "vm[ -]?update|update[ -]?vm|\"vm update\"|\"VM update\"|update script|vm provisioning|cloud vm" . || true

echo
echo "== rg: confirm any reference to bun install/VM update around AGENTS.md =="
rg -n "Dependency refresh|bun install|VM update" AGENTS.md
```

```shell
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

echo "== AGENTS.md: other references to 'update script' / 'VM update' =="
rg -n -i "vm[[:space:]]+update|update[[:space:]]+script" AGENTS.md || true

echo
echo "== Search repo for 'bun.sh/install' (VM provisioning often does this) =="
rg -n --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**' -S "bun\.sh/install|curl -fsSL https://bun\.sh/install" . || true

echo
echo "== Search repo for 'setup-java' / 'jdk' provisioning hints =="
rg -n --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**' -S -i \
  "setup-java|jdk[[:space:]]*21|openjdk|java -version|temurin|adoptopenjdk" . || true

echo
echo "== scripts/: grep for common provisioning/update commands =="
rg -n --hidden --glob '!node_modules/**' --glob '!.git/**' -S -i \
  "apt-get|yum|dnf|apk add|systemctl|curl -fsSL|wget|bun install|nvm|asdf|sdkman|sdkman-init|openjdk|temurin" scripts || true

echo
echo "== Inspect likely 'bootstrap' / 'install' scripts for VM/provision context =="
for f in scripts/openadt-devcontainer-bootstrap scripts/openadt-devcontainer-bootstrap.ps1 scripts/openadt-sdk.ps1 scripts/install-openadt-dev.ps1; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    echo "--- $f (head) ---"
    head -n 80 "$f" || true
  fi
done
```

**Fix broken “VM update script” reference in AGENTS.md**
AGENTS.md says “`bun install` only — see VM update script”, but the repo doesn’t include any “VM update script” (or any other reference resolving that pointer). Add the explicit script path/link (or inline the actual VM dependency-refresh steps).

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@AGENTS.md` around lines 192 - 194, Update the AGENTS.md "Dependency refresh
(update script)" section to remove the broken "see VM update script" pointer and
either add the actual script path/link or inline the VM dependency-refresh
steps; specifically edit the "Dependency refresh (update script)" paragraph to:
(a) state the exact command(s) to run (e.g., `bun install`), (b) include the
PATH instruction to ensure ~/.bun/bin is on PATH (mention adding it to ~/.bashrc
or the appropriate shell rc), and (c) if there is an existing helper script
(create one if missing), add its relative repository path or link and a one-line
usage note so readers can run it directly.
```

</details>
